description: Transition from C to C++ and step into the world of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP).

slides.title: Welcome to C++ ➕➕
slides.content:
# C with Classes 🚀

C++ was developed by **Bjarne Stroustrup** in 1979 at Bell Labs.

## Evolution
He wanted the efficiency of C but with better code organization. He added **Classes**, creating "C with Classes", which later became C++.

## Who uses it?
- **Game Engines:** Unreal Engine, Unity (Core).
- **High-Performance Apps:** Adobe Photoshop, Chrome Browser.
- **Finance:** High-frequency trading platforms.

slides.title: Structure of a C++ Program
Input and Output
slides.content:
# The Stream Team 🌊

## Output: `cout`
Uses `<<` (insertion) to send data to screen.
```cpp
cout << "Age: " << 25 << endl;
```
(`endl` is like `\n` but also flushes the buffer).

## Input: `cin`
Uses `>>` (extraction) to get data from keyboard.
```cpp
int age;
cin >> age;
```
No need for `%d` or `&` (usually)!

slides.title: Namespaces
Introduction to OOP
slides.content:
# Thinking in Objects 💭

**C was Procedural:** Focus on functions (actions).
**C++ is Object-Oriented:** Focus on objects (data + actions).

## Real World Analogy
Instead of just writing code to "drive", we create a **Car** object that has properties (color, speed) and behaviors (accelerate, brake).

slides.title: Class and Object
The 4 Pillars of OOP
slides.content:
# The Foundation 🏛️

1.  **Encapsulation:** Hiding data (using `private`). Protecting different parts of the car engine from the driver.
2.  **Abstraction:** Showing only essentials. Steering wheel is valid, internal combustion physics is hidden.
3.  **Inheritance:** Creating new classes from existing ones. `SportsCar` inherits from `Car`.
4.  **Polymorphism:** Same name, different forms. `move()` can mean drive for a Car, or fly for a Plane.

slides.title: OOP Concept
slides.content: Which OOP concept is about wrapping data and methods into a single unit?

slides.title: Full Code Example
Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: C++ Basics 🎓

Welcome to the modern world!

## Key Takeaways
- **IOStream:** `cout` and `cin` make I/O easier.
- **Namespaces:** Organize code and prevent name clashes.
- **OOP:** A paradigm based on objects and classes.
- **Class:** The blueprint.
- **Object:** The real thing.
- **Pillars:** Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism.

Next, we will look deeper into **Classes and Objects** to build more complex structures!
